Inversion: Think backwards from failure. Avoid stupidity rather than seeking brilliance.
Second-Order Thinking: Think beyond first effects. And then what?
Circle of Competence: Know the boundaries of what you truly understand.
Epistemological Humility: The limits of knowledge. Falsification. What we can and cannot know.
Lollapalooza Effect: Multiple forces combining simultaneously for extreme outcomes.
Antifragility: Systems that gain from disorder, stress, and volatility.
Skin in the Game: Having personal stakes in outcomes. Symmetry of risk and reward.
Optionality & Asymmetric Payoffs: Position for capped downside and uncapped upside.
Survivorship Bias & Narrative Fallacy: We only see winners. We construct stories to explain randomness.
Power Laws & Fat Tails: Extreme outcomes dominate. Most of the value sits in the tail.
Via Negativa: Removal over addition. Subtract the harmful rather than adding the good.
Self-Deception & Confabulation: We lie to ourselves constantly. The narrator in our head fabricates stories.
Incentive Architecture: People respond to incentives, always. Design the incentive, design the behavior.
Status Gradient: Wellbeing is tied to relative position, not absolute wealth.
Radical Acceptance: Accept what is before you can change it. The paradox of change through acceptance.
Compounding: Small advantages accumulating relentlessly over time.
Explore vs Exploit: When to search broadly versus commit and go deep.
Temporal Patience: Spannungsbogen — the self-imposed delay between desire and action.
Enough: Knowing when you have sufficient. The trap of more.
The Resistance: The internal force opposing all creative and meaningful work.
Creative Process & Mastery: The structure of making things. Apprenticeship, craft, deliberate practice.
Flow & Optimal Experience: States of deep engagement where challenge meets skill.
Finite vs Infinite Games: Playing to win versus playing to keep playing.
Remarkability & Differentiation: Stand out or disappear. Be worth talking about.
Systems Over Goals: Build repeatable processes, not just targets. The business is the product.
Private Vices, Public Benefits: Self-interest producing collective good. Emergent order from individual action.
Intangible Capital: Value in what you can't touch — knowledge, brand, network, culture.
Leadership Under Duress: How leaders behave when the situation is worst.
The Unreasonable Man: Progress requires refusal to adapt to the world as it is.
Myth & Meaning-Making: Narratives, archetypes, and belief systems as fundamental human needs.
Stoic Sovereignty: Self-mastery and composure. Governing yourself before governing others.
Pattern & Elegance: Seeking underlying order and beauty in systems and ideas.
